problem: The planning committee at school has 10 members. Exactly four of these members are teachers. A four-person subcommittee with at least one member who is a teacher must be formed from the members of the planning committee. How many distinct subcommittees are possible?

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of members on the planning committee?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many teachers are there on the planning committee?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many non-teachers are there on the planning committee?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we need to consider subcommittees with at least one teacher?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many subcommittees can be formed from the planning committee if there are no restrictions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many subcommittees can be formed from the non-teachers alone?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the number of subcommittees with at least one teacher using the values from the previous questions?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any other conditions or restrictions that we need to consider in this problem?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other scenarios where counting and probability concepts can be applied?
classified_questions.label: e
